title: Resyntha
shortDescription: Multi-Agent Research Intelligence Platform for automated literature review, structured knowledge extraction, and research landscape generation.

overview.problem: Researchers face information overload — manually reviewing hundreds of papers, extracting key findings, and identifying research gaps is slow and inconsistent.
overview.solution: Resyntha orchestrates multiple AI agents across a stage-based pipeline to automate literature reviews: it searches academic sources, extracts structured knowledge, generates research landscapes, detects gaps, and provides an AI copilot for interactive exploration.

architecture: FastAPI backend with a stage-based pipeline engine driving asynchronous execution via ARQ and Redis. SQLAlchemy manages PostgreSQL persistence for versioned artifacts. React + TypeScript frontend with TanStack Query orchestrates real-time pipeline state. Modular agent architecture supports pluggable source adapters for Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, and arXiv. Groq accelerates LLM inference for extraction and analysis stages.
challenges: Building a stage-based pipeline engine with deterministic validation between stages
Managing asynchronous task execution with Redis queue and result backpressure
Orchestrating multi-agent coordination without deadlock or duplicated work
Implementing caching strategies across API sources to respect rate limits
learnings: Deepened understanding of pipeline architecture design, asynchronous task orchestration, multi-agent coordination patterns, and building observability into distributed data workflows.

title: Atlas
shortDescription: Production-grade Retrieval-Augmented Generation platform with OCR pipeline, semantic search, streaming responses, and citation-aware AI.

overview.problem: Traditional search and Q&A systems lack document-grounded, citation-aware responses. Users need answers that are verifiable against source material, not black-box LLM outputs.
overview.solution: Atlas ingests documents through an OCR pipeline, embeds them into pgvector for semantic retrieval, and serves citation-grounded answers with streaming responses. A modular backend separates ingestion, retrieval, generation, and authentication layers.

architecture: Modular FastAPI backend with distinct ingestion, retrieval, generation, and auth services. OCR pipeline normalizes scanned documents before embedding. pgvector enables efficient hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval. Better Auth handles session and API key management. Streaming responses deliver citation-aware AI outputs in real time.
challenges: Designing a modular architecture where ingestion, retrieval, and generation can scale independently
Building an OCR pipeline that normalizes diverse document formats into clean text
Implementing hybrid search (dense + sparse) over pgvector for optimal relevance
Streaming citation-grounded responses without breaking the document context window
learnings: Explored RAG architecture patterns, embedding strategies for domain-specific retrieval, streaming response design, and modular backend decomposition for AI systems.

title: Engineering Portfolio
shortDescription: An engineering-focused portfolio showcasing projects, engineering notes, and an AI assistant using React, Vite, and an Express backend.

overview.problem: Traditional portfolios are static and don't reflect engineering depth or system thinking.
overview.solution: Built a dynamic, JSON/Markdown-driven portfolio with a terminal-inspired UI and an AI assistant that understands project and note context.

architecture: React + Vite frontend, Express backend providing AI and content APIs. Content is driven by JSON and Markdown files for easy updates.
challenges: Designing a reusable content pipeline for JSON and Markdown
Rendering engineering notes safely with syntax highlighting
Integrating a lightweight AI assistant without overengineering
learnings: Improved content architecture, markdown rendering, and clean backend integration for AI-assisted interactions.

title: SUMS - Smart University Management System
shortDescription: Role-based university management system focusing on academic workflows and placement processes.

overview.problem: University administrative workflows were fragmented and required role-based access control and transactional integrity.
overview.solution: Designed normalized PostgreSQL schemas and role-based APIs to support Students, Faculty, and Admin workflows reliably.

architecture: Express.js backend, PostgreSQL with normalized schemas, clear role-based routing and validation middleware.
challenges: Designing a BCNF-like normalized schema that supports role-based workflows
Implementing transactional operations and validation across modules
learnings: Database normalization practices, API design for role-based systems, and validation pipelines.

title: School Management API
shortDescription: REST APIs for managing school data with geolocation-based sorting and basic reporting.

overview.problem: Schools require structured APIs for student, staff, and location-based queries.
overview.solution: Implemented CRUD APIs with geolocation features and documented endpoints for maintainable backend workflows.

architecture: Express.js backend with MySQL, simple reporting endpoints and Postman-documented APIs.
learnings: Practical API design, geolocation querying, and deployment basics.
